description: Target table for the bridge-backed reference campaign. It mixes toy-placeholder controls, internal derived-synthetic checks, and one stronger evidence-grade benchmark anchored to a real imported repository dataset path rather than to direct experiment. This is still not real-world physics evidence, but it is strong enough to separate control-study success from internal benchmark mismatch.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-2-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-3-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-1-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-2-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-ratio-derived-synthetic
targets.notes: Derived-synthetic internal benchmark from the executable SU(2) toy model; useful for checking whether the structured-environment observable stays near the analytic reference.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-1-ratio-small-sample
targets.notes: Small-sample control using a Student-t tail model instead of a Gaussian one.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-2-imported-repo-benchmark
targets.notes: Stronger internal benchmark tied to the imported repository mesh side of the campaign. It is evidence-grade within the repository contract because it is not a toy placeholder, but it is still not an external measurement or outside dataset.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-external-lattice-gauge-benchmark
targets.notes: Phase XI M2: genuine external benchmark target anchored to the external lattice gauge imported environment (datasetId=external-lattice-gauge-su2-2d-v1). This is the first external-benchmark class target in the campaign. The scorecard BenchmarkClassCounts will now explicitly list both 'internal-benchmark' and 'external-benchmark' as distinct categories, satisfying the D-P11-002 scorecard separation requirement.
